title: Bound DODAG state and apply config
description: Complete Rust DIO configuration handling exposed during RPL recovery: advertise and consume MinHopRankIncrease/Lifetime Unit consistently; derive root Rank from active configuration; validate a newer-version DIO rank before clearing parents; bound candidate-parent state and remove entries when neighbor eviction occurs.
acceptance_criteria: Literal tests cover non-256 MinHopRankIncrease root/join behavior, Lifetime Unit propagation, inadmissible newer-version DIO preserving current state, parent capacity exhaustion, and neighbor eviction removing DODAG parent state; node/RPL tests and three reviews pass.
notes: Reopened 2026-07-18 after recovery audit found neighbor eviction did not remove DODAG parent state. Final implementation synchronizes bounded neighbor/DODAG state transactionally; protects preferred parents during churn; batch-prunes stale parents; validates complete DIO bytes/options, ETX, rank config, lollipop versions, poison, and capacity before mutation; supports configured roots and measured-link RPL ingress. Final gates: cargo test -p lichen-rpl --all-features PASS (96 unit + 5 integration); cargo test -p lichen-node --features std PASS (155 unit, 1 doctest, 1 ignored); cargo clippy -p lichen-rpl --all-features --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; cargo clippy -p lichen-node --features std --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings -A dead-code PASS with only pre-existing scheduler dead-code allowance; changed-file rustfmt and git diff --check PASS. Three independent exact-diff reviews (correctness, adversarial security, acceptance/API) reported no code findings. Residual u32 age ambiguity is tracked as project-LICHEN-2auf.44.10; workspace/node-wide unrelated gates remain tracked under .60.
close_reason: Completed literal acceptance tests, full scoped gates, and three independent post-reopen reviews with no code findings.
